mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising consumer prices and persistently low policy rates creates stagflationary expectations and forces delayed aggressive tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample regression, or 20-day returns are positive when both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening supply-side capacity and rising corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day returns with Rank IC below 0.01 during expansionary periods, or returns are consistently negative despite industrial production acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real short-term consumer credit reflect expanding household purchasing power and consumption demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth shows no correlation with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC below 0.02) across multiple market cycles, or returns are negative during credit expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1.5 million units signals collapsing construction demand and broader economic weakness, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances where HOUST falls below 1500.0, or the threshold signal produces a Rank IC below 0.01 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields insignificant predictive power (Rank IC below 0.015) for 20-day returns, or returns are negative during periods of joint labor market improvement.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real gross domestic product confirms sustained economic expansion and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during healthy growth regimes.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict 60-day returns (Rank IC below 0.02) during expansionary periods, or forward returns are negative despite positive GDP growth.
